description: Editorial checks for source-book extraction and course data. These rules catch known typos, OCR artifacts, dated learner patterns, and variants that should not become primary training material.

rules.recommendation: Use the modern closed form `weekdays`; in phrases use `on weekdays`.
Likely typo. Use `week`, `weekdays`, or `weekends` depending on context.
Do not teach this as a default phrase for makeup. Use `put on makeup` or `do one's makeup`.
For `–≤ –ø–æ—Å–ª–µ–¥–Ω—é—é –º–∏–Ω—É—Ç—É`, use `to come at the last minute`. `In time` means before it is too late.
Use `to clean the room` or `to tidy the room`.
For a general restaurant, use `at a restaurant` or `in a restaurant`. Use `the` only when the restaurant is specific/known.
`Supper` is possible but dated/regional for many learners. Prefer `dinner` as primary; keep `supper` only as an accepted alternative when appropriate.
`Seldom` is correct but formal. Prefer `rarely` as the modern primary beginner pattern; accept `seldom` where the book uses it.
For Russian `—É–º—ã–≤–∞—Ç—å—Å—è`, prefer `wash`, `wash one's face`, or a concrete routine phrase. `Wash themselves` is grammatical but often unnatural as a default.
OCR artifact: Cyrillic `–∞` appears inside an English phrase. Replace with Latin `a`.
OCR artifact: Cyrillic `–¢` in `TV`. Use Latin `TV`.
OCR/source artifact around `on time`. Verify against the PDF image and normalize to `on time`.
Acceptable AmE. If lesson is British-first, display `at weekends` as primary and accept `on weekends` as an alternative.
